title: papaver alpinum subsp. rhaeticum
intro: Papaver alpinum subsp. rhaeticum, also known as the Alpine Poppy, is a subspecies of the Papaver alpinum plant, which is a flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). The Alpine Poppy has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as papaverine and thebaine. The health benefits of Papaver alpinum subsp. rhaeticum include its potential to treat various conditions, including pain relief, fever reduction, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the Alpine Poppy include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
